# Switch

Turns an immediate setting on or off.

## Installation

Add the Switch components from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/switch
```

## Usage

Use Switch for a binary setting that takes effect when the control is changed, such as enabling notifications. Bind `v-model` when application state owns the value, and label the setting itself rather than writing an action such as “Turn notifications on.”

**Switch example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex items-center space-x-2">
    <Switch id="airplane-mode" />
    <Label for="airplane-mode">Airplane mode</Label>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Label } from '@/Components/Label'
import { Switch } from '@/Components/Switch'
</script>
```

## Labels

Every switch needs an accessible name. With separate components, match the Label's `for` to the Switch's `id` so the label also enlarges the hit area. `SwitchControl` is a convenience wrapper that generates this association and forwards the root props and model event.

**Switch control example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex flex-col items-start gap-2">
    <SwitchControl v-model="enabled">Notifications</SwitchControl>
    <p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      {{ enabled ? 'Notifications are enabled.' : 'Notifications are disabled.' }}
    </p>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { SwitchControl } from '@/Components/Switch'

const enabled = ref(false)
</script>
```

## Controlled values

The ordinary model is boolean. The root also forwards Reka's `true-value` and `false-value` props when the application deliberately uses another pair of values. Keep the value type consistent and avoid translating display labels into state.

```vue
<SwitchControl v-model="notificationsEnabled">
  Notifications
</SwitchControl>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'

const notificationsEnabled = ref(false)
</script>
```

## Pending and disabled state

If changing the switch saves remotely, update it optimistically only when failure can be clearly reversed. Otherwise disable the control while saving and show a nearby status. A disabled switch still needs an explanation when the reason is not obvious; do not use disabled state as the only error message.

## Accessibility

The switch exposes its checked state through the underlying Reka control and supports keyboard activation. Do not repeat “on” or “off” in the label because assistive technology announces the state. Keep any content in the thumb slot decorative, and do not rely on its position or color as the only indication of state.

## Choosing a binary control

> **Use Checkbox for a submitted choice: [Checkbox](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/checkbox)**
>
> Use Checkbox when a yes-or-no choice is reviewed and submitted with a form. Use Switch when the setting changes immediately after activation.

Use Toggle for a temporary interface mode such as bold formatting, and Button for a command that happens once. A success or failure state belongs in Badge, Alert, or another status surface rather than in a Switch.

Hosted documentation: [Switch](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/switch)
